steps.title: Find the base angles
steps.content:
Since $AC=CD$, triangle $ACD$ is isosceles. Its vertex angle is $108^\circ$, so each base angle is:
$$\frac{180^\circ-108^\circ}{2}=36^\circ$$

steps.title: Find the adjacent angle
steps.content:
Angles $ACD$ and $ACB$ form a linear pair, so:
$$180^\circ-108^\circ=72^\circ$$
In triangle $ABC$, the other two angles therefore sum to:
$$180^\circ-72^\circ=108^\circ$$

steps.title: Use triangle ABE
steps.content:
In triangle $ABE$, angle $BAE$ is $36^\circ$ more than angle $BAC$, and angle $ABE$ is $29^\circ$ less than angle $ABC$. Therefore:
$$x+108^\circ+36^\circ-29^\circ=180^\circ$$
$$x+115^\circ=180^\circ$$
$$x=65$$
The answer is 65.
